name: feature-probe
$comment: Phase 6 wire shape: _action carries {name} only. Parameters that used to ride in `context` (redirect URL, storage values, download URL/filename, form note, sort column/direction, table filter, page number) now live in dedicated state slots on FeatureProbeState (Plan 06-04). The fixture writes them via stateMutations to mimic the renderer's bind writes that would happen before each user click. Table sort uses per-column action names (table-sort-name / table-sort-status); pagination uses prevAction/nextAction (table-page-prev / table-page-next). 1.11.0: buildVm now also renders a layout:"row" section (with LinkNode children); it is static view-shape captured by every GET step (initial/fresh-*), so the byte-identical diff across .NET/bun/node covers the new SectionNode field without a dedicated action step. 1.12.0: buildVm additionally renders the arrange/align alignment vocabulary as static view-shape — a bare layout:"row" section with NEITHER arrange nor align (proving omitted = absent on the wire), the canonical header-bar (row + arrange:"space-between" + a heading TextNode first child + a nested row nav cluster, ALIGN-04), one row per remaining arrange value (start/center/end/space-around/space-evenly), and one row per align value (start/center/end/stretch/baseline). All captured by the existing GET steps, so the byte-identical diff covers PageNode/SectionNode.arrange + .align with no new step. 1.13.0: buildVm additionally renders the switcher vocabulary as static view-shape — a bare layout:"switcher" section with NEITHER threshold nor limit (proving omitted = absent on the wire), one switcher per threshold value (sm/md/lg/xl), and a switcher with limit:4 set (6 LinkNode children). All captured by the existing GET steps, so the byte-identical diff covers PageNode/SectionNode.layout:"switcher" + .threshold + .limit with no new step. 1.13.0 also: buildVm renders the cards minItem vocabulary as static view-shape — a SECTION-level bare layout:"cards" section with NO minItem (proving omitted = absent on the wire, even though the page root is already layout:"cards"), and one layout:"cards" section per minItem value (xs/sm/md/lg/xl). All captured by the existing GET steps, so the byte-identical diff covers PageNode/SectionNode.minItem with no new step. Phase 10 (fits node): buildVm additionally renders the new fits node as static view-shape — a fits with `axis` OMITTED (proving omitted = absent on the wire) and a fits with `axis:"both"` (proving the axis field present as the JSON string "both"), each wrapped in a labeled section and carrying preferred-first/fallback-last candidate children (a layout:"row" candidate first, a layout:"stack" candidate last). All captured by the existing GET steps, so the byte-identical diff covers the {type:"fits", axis?, children} wire with no new step. NOTE: the fits node's CLIENT-SIDE measure-and-pick selection is browser-only (ResizeObserver + DOM layout in BrowserAdapter) and is NOT part of parity — parity proves only that the node serializes identically across backends. 2.1.0 (LinkNode.active): the canonical header-bar nav cluster marks its first link active:true ("you are here") in both backends; the remaining links omit active (proving omitted = absent on the wire, matching the .NET bool?+WhenWritingNull posture). Captured by the existing GET steps, so the byte-identical diff covers LinkNode.active with no new step. 3.0.0 (unified appearance axes): buildVm renders an "Appearance axes" section as static view-shape covering the new vocabulary — button emphasis/tone/size (+ a primary×danger×lg combo and a copy-button with all three), section tone (a card+tone and a bare tone), text tone (+ heading×tone), list-item state+tone, and table-row state+tone. The old fused `variant` is gone (buttons→emphasis/tone, list/row→state/tone, text style→tone). Captured by the existing GET steps, so the byte-identical diff covers every new field with no new step. 3.2.0 (child-side modifiers): buildVm renders a "Child modifiers (alignSelf + maxWidth)" section as static view-shape — a bare variant:"card" child (NEITHER alignSelf nor maxWidth => proves omitted = absent on the wire), one card per alignSelf value (start/center/end), one card per maxWidth value (half/two-thirds/three-quarters/prose), and a two-bubble chat composition (alignSelf + maxWidth + tone). Captured by the existing GET steps, so the byte-identical diff covers SectionNode.alignSelf + .maxWidth with no new step. Feedback primitives: buildVm renders a 'Feedback primitives' section as static view-shape — three BadgeNodes (bare => tone/emphasis absent, tone-only, tone+emphasis) and two EmptyStateNodes (bare => message/action absent, and one with message + a CTA ButtonNode whose unique action name feedback-cta is reached by the action-name walk descending into empty-state.action). Captured by the existing GET steps. The new trigger-toast POST step returns TWO toast side-effects (a bare message-only toast and a full tone+durationMs toast), byte-diffing the {type:'toast', message, tone?, durationMs?} side-effect wire across all backends (the toast's client-side display is browser-only and NOT part of parity). 4.0.0 (file uploadOn routing): the File-field section renders a second file field 'upload-routed' with uploadOn:["probe-submit"] alongside the bare 'upload-nobind' (proving uploadOn omitted = absent on the wire vs. present as a JSON string array). Captured by the existing GET steps, so the byte-identical diff covers FieldNode.uploadOn with no new step (the browser's uploadOn-based file collection is browser-only and NOT part of parity). Phase 12 / reshaped Phase 18 (CHARTBASE-04, chart node): buildVm additionally renders a `chart (bar)` section with two ChartNodes over the reshaped multi-series wire {type:"chart", kind?, labels, series:[{name,data,tone?}], stacked?, title?}. The first ChartNode carries `kind` OMITTED (proving omitted = absent on the wire, default "bar"), shared `labels` (Mon/Tue/Wed), two series over WHOLE-NUMBER data so double/number serialize byte-identically (12 not 12.0) — "Visits" (12/19/7, no tone, framework-assigned palette slot) and "Errors" (1/3/2, tone:"danger", a semantic override) — `stacked:true`, and a title ("Weekly visits"). The second ChartNode sets `kind:"line"` explicitly (proving the literal string crosses the wire) with a single series ("Trend", 5/10/15) and stacked/title omitted. Captured by the existing GET steps, so the byte-identical diff covers the reshaped labels/series/tone/stacked wire with no new step. NOTE: the ChartNode's CLIENT-SIDE Chart.js pixels (bars/colors/title/legend) are browser-only and NOT part of parity — parity proves only that the node serializes identically across backends. Phase 14 (NBA-04): buildVm additionally renders a 'Non-blocking actions (blocking field)' section as static view-shape — a button whose action OMITS blocking (proves omitted = absent on the wire) and a button whose action sets blocking:false (proves the literal JSON boolean false). All captured by the existing GET steps, so the byte-identical diff covers ActionEvent.blocking/ActionDescriptor.Blocking with no new step. NOTE: the CLIENT-SIDE coalescing/epoch behavior this field enables is NOT parity-tested (pure client-only mechanics per the design of record — no wire epoch, no server-side reconciliation state); parity proves only that the blocking field itself serializes identically across backends. Jump-to-page (MQ4-01): buildTableSection additionally sets Pagination.JumpAction ("table-page-jump"), exercised by two new POST steps after tbl-filter-clear — tbl-page-jump-valid (a valid direct jump to the last page, 3) and tbl-page-jump-clamp (an agent supplying an out-of-range page, 99, directly to the tablePage bind) — proving the pre-existing server-side clamp (Math.Clamp/Math.min+Math.max in Window()/tableWindow()) renders the same clamped page identically across dotnet-probe, bun-probe, and node-probe. v5.0 (ButtonNode.confirm): the Appearance-axes button list renders a 'confirm-guard' ButtonNode carrying tone:"danger" + confirm:"Delete this? This cannot be undone." (a destructive-action guard). Captured by the existing GET steps, so the byte-identical diff covers ButtonNode.confirm (WhenWritingNull => omitted absent on the wire). NOTE: the CLIENT-SIDE native window.confirm() gate this field triggers is browser-only and NOT part of parity — parity proves only that the confirm string serializes identically across backends. v5.1 (NAV-01/NAV-02, BreadcrumbNode + StepsNode): buildVm additionally renders a 'Navigation primitives' section as static view-shape — a breadcrumb whose crumbs cover the full omitted-vs-present matrix (an href-only crumb with external OMITTED => absent, an external:true crumb present as the literal boolean, an action crumb whose UNIQUE name nav-crumb-probe proves the action-name uniqueness walk DESCENDS into breadcrumb items, and a final label-only crumb the framework auto-renders as the current page), plus two StepsNodes — the first OMITS orientation (proves absent = default horizontal) and mixes a description-bearing step with two bare ones (description omitted => absent), the second sets orientation:"vertical" (proves the literal string crosses) — both with a mid current:1 (0 is meaningful, so current always crosses). All captured by the existing GET steps, so the byte-identical diff covers the {type:"breadcrumb", items:[{label, href?, external?, action?}]} and {type:"steps", steps:[{label, description?}], current, orientation?} wire with no new POST step. NOTE: the CLIENT-SIDE appearance/a11y (separators, numbered markers, connector lines, intrinsic horizontal→vertical reflow, aria-current) is browser-only and NOT part of parity — parity proves only that the two nodes serialize identically across backends. v5.2 (LOOK-01/LOOK-06, FieldNode lookup + lookup-multiple): buildVm additionally renders a 'Lookup field' section as static view-shape — three FieldNodes covering the full omitted-vs-present matrix. lookup-owner is THE HEADLINE: selected is PRESENT while candidates is ABSENT (the preselected-value/cold-start case — the label crosses on the NODE and is never resolved out of an empty candidate list), and allowCustom is OMITTED => absent on the wire, not false (the WhenWritingDefault posture on the .NET side, matching the TS optional bool); its selected entry carries label + type (the polymorphic-ref tag crosses). lookup-tag sets allowCustom:true (present as the literal boolean) alongside candidates, and its selected entry OMITS label because it equals value (the free-form-tag case) and OMITS type (a monomorphic ref). lookup-watchers is a lookup-multiple with TWO selected entries and a bind pointing at a string[] of ids, carrying searchBind plus a searchAction whose UNIQUE name lookup-search-probe proves the action-name uniqueness walk DESCENDS into FieldNode.searchAction on BOTH backends (never POSTed by any step — pure static wire-shape proof, same convention as the axes-noop-* / nba-* / nav-crumb-probe names). All captured by the existing GET steps, so the byte-identical diff covers the {type:"field", inputType:"lookup"|"lookup-multiple", bind, selected?:[{value, label?, type?}], candidates?:[{value, label?, type?}], searchBind?, searchAction?, allowCustom?} wire with no new POST step. NOTE: the CLIENT-SIDE debounce, popup/listbox, chips, live-region announcements, and the non-blocking lane's coalescing/epoch behavior are browser-only and NOT part of parity — parity proves only that the lookup wire serializes identically across backends. 6.1.0 (ListNode.ordered): the Appearance-axes section renders a SECOND list with ordered:true (an <ol>) directly after the existing unordered list (which OMITS ordered, proving absent = <ul> on the wire). Captured by the existing GET steps, so the byte-identical diff covers ListNode.ordered with no new step; the initial step's expectBodyContains asserts \"ordered\":true crosses (a coverage tripwire so the branch can't go vacuous). NOTE: the CLIENT-SIDE <ol> rendering + the CSS-counter numbering is browser-only and NOT part of parity — parity proves only that the ordered field serializes identically across backends. RICH COPY (npm 6.x / NuGet 6.x): the install-command CopyButtonNode now also carries html (server-provided text/html rep), and the Appearance-axes axes-clip CopyButtonNode carries copyTargetId pointing at the sibling "Warning card" SectionNode (now given id:"axes-warning-card", emitted as a DOM id for the harvest route). Both new optional string fields + the section id are static view-shape on the initial GET, so the byte-identical diff covers them across .NET/bun/node with no new step; the initial step's expectBodyContains asserts "copyTargetId":"axes-warning-card" and "html":"<code> cross (coverage tripwires so the branch can't go vacuous). NOTE: the actual clipboard write (ClipboardItem text/html + text/plain) + the DOM harvest are browser-only and NOT part of parity — parity proves only that the fields serialize identically across backends. TRACKER (npm 6.x / NuGet 6.x, TrackerNode): buildVm renders a 'Status tracker' section with one TrackerNode covering the omitted-vs-present matrix — a cell with state OMITTED (absent = muted default), one cell per state (success/danger/warning/muted), a labeled cell, and a cell whose action name tracker-cell-probe is UNIQUE, proving the action-name uniqueness walk DESCENDS into TrackerCell.action on BOTH backends (never POSTed — pure static wire-shape proof). Captured by the existing GET steps; the initial step's expectBodyContains asserts "type":"tracker", "id":"probe-tracker", "state":"warning" (proves the TrackerState enum kebab-serializes on .NET) and tracker-cell-probe cross (coverage tripwires so the branch can't go vacuous). NOTE: the client-side appearance (baked colorblind-safe palette, hairline gap, shrink-then-scroll overflow, hover/tooltip, keyboard activation) is browser-only and NOT part of parity — parity proves only that the {type:"tracker", id?, cells:[{state?, label?, action?}]} wire serializes identically across backends. 6.10.0 (CodeBlockNode): buildVm additionally renders THREE code-blocks after the task-list — a BARE code-block (only `code` set, proving language/filename/copyable OMITTED cross as absent, WhenWritingNull posture), a FULL one with `code:"def add(a, b):\n    return a + b"` + `language:"python"` + `filename:"add.py"` (proving the strings + the multi-line code cross the wire byte-identically including the \n), and a `copyable:false` block (proving the JSON `false` boolean crosses — meaningful, not a default). The initial step's expectBodyContains asserts `"type":"code-block"`, `"language":"python"`, `"filename":"add.py"`, `"copyable":false`, and the `def add(a, b)` substring cross (coverage tripwires so the branch can't go vacuous — if a backend serializes the boolean as a string, drops the code entirely, or emits `null` for absent, the step fails LOUDLY). NOTE: the CLIENT-SIDE <pre><code> emission + the header row (filename + language badge + copy button) is browser-only and NOT part of parity — parity proves only that the node shape serializes identically across backends. 6.10.0 (ListItemNode.completed): buildVm additionally renders a small three-item list after the blockquote section — one item with `completed:true`, one with `completed:false`, and one with `completed` OMITTED — proving JSON `true` and JSON `false` literals cross the wire as bare booleans AND that omitted = absent (WhenWritingNull ⇒ absent, not null) on BOTH backends. The initial step's expectBodyContains asserts `"completed":true` and `"completed":false` cross AND the plain-item text `"Plain item (no marker)"` renders (proves the completed-omitted branch actually runs), so if a backend serializes the boolean as a string, drops it, or emits `null` for absent, the step fails LOUDLY instead of going vacuous. NOTE: the CLIENT-SIDE ☑/☐ glyph rendering + the .vms-list-item--task-done strike-through is browser-only and NOT part of parity — parity proves only that the completed field serializes identically across backends. 6.10.0 (BlockquoteNode): buildVm additionally renders TWO blockquotes after the heading-levels section — a bare blockquote holding one paragraph (proves the {type:"blockquote", children[]} wire serializes byte-identically) and a NESTED blockquote (proves recursive children work), whose inner children include a ButtonNode with the UNIQUE action name `blockquote-action-probe` proving the action-name uniqueness walk DESCENDS into BlockquoteNode.Children on BOTH backends (never POSTed — pure static wire-shape proof, same convention as fits-blindspot / nav-crumb-probe / tracker-cell-probe). The initial step's expectBodyContains asserts `"type":"blockquote"` and the probe action name cross (coverage tripwires so the branch can't go vacuous). NOTE: the CLIENT-SIDE <blockquote> HTML emission + the indented/accent-bar styling is browser-only and NOT part of parity — parity proves only that the node shape serializes identically across backends. 6.10.0 (TextNode.level): buildVm additionally renders seven heading-level TextNodes after the captioned images — one per valid level 1..6 (proving the integer crosses as a bare JSON number 1..6 on both backends) plus a level:2 + tone:"danger" combination (proving Level composes with Tone as two orthogonal axes). The pre-existing TextNodes throughout the fixture OMIT level (WhenWritingNull ⇒ absent on the wire, not null). The initial step's expectBodyContains asserts "level":1 and "level":6 cross as bare integers plus the H1 and H2-danger value strings, so if a backend serializes level as a string or drops it entirely the step fails LOUDLY instead of going vacuous. NOTE: the CLIENT-SIDE emission of the real <h1>-<h6> HTML tag is browser-only and NOT part of parity — parity proves only that the level field serializes identically across backends. 6.10.0 (ImageNode.caption + ImageNode.captionRuns): buildVm additionally renders TWO captioned images after the existing bare avatar image — the first with plain `caption:"Figure 1: the framework logo"` (captionRuns OMITTED, proving absent = null on the wire per the WhenWritingNull posture) and the second with `caption:"See the docs"` + `captionRuns:[{text:"See "}, {text:"the docs", href, external}]` (proving the InlineRun[] alongside caption crosses byte-identically). Captured by the existing GET steps, so the byte-identical diff covers ImageNode.caption + .captionRuns with no new step; the initial step's expectBodyContains asserts both caption strings + `"captionRuns":[` cross (coverage tripwires so the branch can't go vacuous). NOTE: the CLIENT-SIDE <figure>/<figcaption> emission is browser-only and NOT part of parity — parity proves only that the two new fields serialize identically across backends. DIFF (npm 6.x / NuGet 6.x, DiffNode): buildVm renders a 'Diff' section with TWO DiffNodes covering the full omitted-vs-present matrix. The first is a bare diff with mode/header/id ALL omitted (proves side-by-side default is absent, header absent, id absent — three separate WhenWritingNull posture checks). Its rows exercise every SHAPE-carries-meaning kind: a context row (both old+new present with identical text + lineNumber:1/2/3 — proves the shape-carries-meaning contract's context arm), a pure-remove row (new:null => ABSENT on the wire, NOT null — gotcha #8, the specific class-2 defect findNulls catches per-response), a pure-add row (old:null => absent), a modified pair (both non-null with different text — side-by-side renders both tinted; unified would split remove+add), and a prose row with NO lineNumber on either side (lineNumber:null => absent — the docs-diff use case). The second DiffNode sets mode:"unified" + header:{old,new} + id:"probe-diff-unified" so all three optional fields cross the wire as literal values. DiffNode is action-free (collectActions / Collect fall through the same way as ChartNode / StepsNode); nothing to prove for uniqueness descent. Captured by the existing GET steps; the initial step's expectBodyContains asserts "type":"diff", "id":"probe-diff-unified", "mode":"unified", the header shape, and a lineNumber value cross (coverage tripwires so the branch can't go vacuous). NOTE: the CLIENT-SIDE appearance (CSS-Grid alignment, tint+stripe row coloring, long-line horizontal-scroll-per-cell, unified-mode linenum-column collapse) is browser-only and NOT part of parity — parity proves only that the {type:"diff", id?, rows:[{old?:{text,lineNumber?}, new?:{text,lineNumber?}}], mode?, header?:{old,new}} wire serializes identically across backends.

steps.$comment: expectBodyContains is a COVERAGE tripwire (like helpdesk-seeded): the stat-bar renders as static shape here, and "value":"12" asserts the stat value crosses as a JSON STRING, not a bare number — the exact cross-backend drift the byte-diff never saw because no fixture rendered stat-bar at all before 6.0.0. "stat-bar" + the toned failing tile prove the node and StatItem.tone are exercised; if someone reverts value to a number this step fails LOUDLY instead of going vacuous.
